title: story-15-review-churn
description:
This is a continuation of the broader review-churn pattern. In the current window, story 15 completed another successful reviewer run at 2026-05-29T01:18:44.153461+00:00, and the flagged watcher note reports that the story is now active at 13 cumulative reviewer cycles. There are no failed runs, retry clusters, prompt-placeholder leaks, or spend-spike signals in this window; the concern is structural non-convergence rather than an execution failure.

The downstream impact is continued cost burn and queue occupancy without advancing the story out of review. The queue snapshot at 2026-05-29T01:18:27.105718+00:00 shows 9 stories in reviewer_requested_changes, consistent with ongoing review backlog, though not a full queue stall. A plausible explanation is that the dev/reviewer loop for story 15 is repeatedly succeeding at the run level while failing to converge at the workflow level. This pattern already meets the review-churn escalation threshold by a wide margin, and the new reviewer completion is changed evidence that the churn is still active rather than historical.

evidence.summary_excerpt: Story 15 completed another reviewer cycle and is now active at 13 cumulative reviewer cycles, indicating continued non-converging review churn.

evidence.excerpt: {"noteworthy":"Story 15 is active in this window with 13 reviewer cycles, which is far beyond the escalation threshold; story 19 is no longer active."}
successful reviewer run for story 15, attempt_n=13, duration_s=15.541, cost_usd=0.037721

escalation_reason: Review churn on story 15 remains active and has reached 13 reviewer cycles, with another reviewer completion at 2026-05-29T01:18:44.153461+00:00. L3 judgment is needed because this is sustained non-convergence well beyond the 3-cycle ceiling, causing continued cumulative cost burn and workflow blockage despite successful individual runs.
